back_translation: Brothers, the desire of my heart-mind and the petition-prayer to God for them is for their salvation
For I bear witness about them that they have zeal for God — but not according to true knowledge
Because they did not know the righteousness of God, and sought to establish their own — they did not submit to the righteousness of God
For Christ (royally) is the end of the Law, leading to righteousness for everyone who believes
For Moses writes concerning the righteousness from the Law: 'The person who does these things will live by them'
But the righteousness from faith speaks like this: 'Do not say in your heart-mind: "Who will ascend to the heavens?"' — that is, in order to bring Christ down
Or 'Who will descend into the abyss?' — that is, in order to bring Christ up from the dead
But what does it say? 'The word is near you — in your mouth and in your heart-mind' — that is, the word of faith which we proclaim
If you confess with your mouth that 'Jesus (royally) is the Lord' and believe in your heart-mind that God (royally) raised Him from the dead, you will be saved
For with the heart-mind, one believes leading to righteousness — and with the mouth, one confesses leading to salvation
For the Scripture says: 'Everyone who believes in Him will not be disappointed at all'
For there is no difference between Jew and Greek — for the same Lord is Lord over all — (royally) giving riches to all who call on His (royal) name
For 'Everyone who calls upon the (royal) name of the Lord will be saved'
Therefore, how will they call upon the One whom they have not believed in? And how will they believe in the One whom they have not heard? And how will they hear without a proclaimer?
And how will they proclaim if they are not sent? Just as it is written: 'How beautiful are the feet of those who proclaim good news'
But not all obeyed the good news — for Isaiah says: 'Lord, who has believed what we have heard?'
Therefore, faith comes from hearing — and hearing comes through the (royal) word of Christ
But I ask: did they not hear? Indeed they did — 'Their voice has gone out into all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world'
I ask again: did Israel not understand? Moses speaks first: 'I will provoke you to jealousy by those who are not a nation — by a nation lacking understanding I will provoke you to anger'
And Isaiah speaks boldly: 'I was found by those who did not seek Me — I appeared to those who did not ask for Me'
But to Israel He (royally) speaks: 'All day long I have stretched out My hands toward a people who are disobedient and contradicting'
